question: [File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkStakeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: findLatest()] Can an unprivileged external user repeatedly poll GET /api/v1/network/stake to read the current unreservedStakingRewardBalance and stakingRewardRate fields returned by findLatest(), then time a staking transaction to claim rewards just before the balance is exhausted, effectively stealing rewards from other stakers who submitted later?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkStakeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: findLatest()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that findLatest() uses a correlated subquery (select max(consensus_timestamp) from network_stake) with no row-level locking, creating a TOCTOU window where the returned NetworkStake row reflects a stale rewardBalanceThreshold, causing the system to incorrectly activate staking rewards and allow the attacker to claim unpaid rewards?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkStakeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: findLatest()] Can an unprivileged external user observe that findLatest() returns the row with the maximum consensus_timestamp and, by monitoring the /api/v1/network/stake endpoint, detect the exact moment a new staking period begins (stakingPeriod changes), then front-run reward claims before the unreservedStakingRewardBalance is updated, stealing rewards that should be distributed to other accounts?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkStakeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: findLatest()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of any authentication or rate limiting on the GET /api/v1/network/stake endpoint backed by findLatest() to perform high-frequency polling, extracting precise unreservedStakingRewardBalance values to calculate the exact remaining unpaid staking rewards and drain them before other legitimate stakers?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkStakeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: findLatest()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that findLatest() returns a NetworkStake entity where isNew() always returns true (to avoid Hibernate pre-insert queries), causing duplicate NetworkStake rows to be inserted with the same consensus_timestamp, resulting in the max(consensus_timestamp) subquery returning an ambiguous row and exposing incorrect stakingRewardRate values that inflate reward calculations?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkStakeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: findLatest()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the NetworkStakeMapper's division operation (mapFraction(source.getStakingRewardFeeNumerator(), source.getStakingRewardFeeDenominator())) when stakingRewardFeeDenominator is zero in the row returned by findLatest(), causing an ArithmeticException or returning an infinite/NaN fraction that bypasses fee deductions and allows the attacker to receive full staking rewards without the expected fee reduction?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkStakeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: findLatest()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the NetworkStakeMapper's division operation (mapFraction(source.getNodeRewardFeeNumerator(), source.getNodeRewardFeeDenominator())) when nodeRewardFeeDenominator is zero in the row returned by findLatest(), causing a divide-by-zero that results in incorrect node reward fee fractions, allowing the attacker to claim a larger share of unpaid staking rewards than entitled?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkStakeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: findLatest()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that findLatest() uses select * with a native query, causing all fields including reservedStakingRewards and unreservedStakingRewardBalance to be exposed in the API response, allowing the attacker to precisely calculate the total unpaid reward pool and craft transactions to claim the maximum possible rewards before other stakers?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkStakeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: findLatest()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a stale cache of the findLatest() result (if caching is applied at the service layer) where the cached NetworkStake shows a higher unreservedStakingRewardBalance than the actual current value, causing the system to over-distribute staking rewards and allowing the attacker to claim rewards that exceed the actual available balance?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkStakeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: findLatest()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the long data type overflow in maxStakingRewardRatePerHbar (stored as Java long) when the value approaches Long.MAX_VALUE, causing the reward rate calculation to wrap around to a negative or zero value, resulting in incorrect reward distributions that the attacker can exploit to claim disproportionate unpaid staking rewards?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkStakeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: findLatest()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the long data type overflow in stakingRewardRate (stored as Java long) when multiplied by stakeTotal in downstream reward calculations, causing integer overflow that produces an incorrect total reward amount, allowing the attacker to claim more unpaid staking rewards than the system should distribute?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkStakeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: findLatest()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the epochDay field returned by findLatest() being off by one (due to the importer subtracting 1 from the epoch day), causing downstream reward period calculations to reference the wrong staking period and allowing the attacker to claim rewards for a period they did not participate in?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkStakeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: findLatest()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that findLatest() returns an Optional<NetworkStake> that is unwrapped in NetworkServiceImpl.getLatestNetworkStake() with orElseThrow(), and by triggering conditions that empty the network_stake table (e.g., through a pruning race condition), cause an EntityNotFoundException that disrupts reward distribution and allows the attacker to claim rewards during the error recovery window?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkStakeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: findLatest()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the stakingStartThreshold field exposed by findLatest() to determine the exact minimum balance of account 0.0.800 required to activate staking rewards, then monitor the balance approaching this threshold and time reward claims to maximize unpaid reward extraction at the moment rewards activate?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkStakeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: findLatest()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the rewardBalanceThreshold field exposed by findLatest() to determine the exact threshold below which the staking reward rate is reduced, then time staking transactions to claim maximum rewards just before the unreservedStakingRewardBalance drops below this threshold, stealing rewards at the full rate before the reduction kicks in?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkStakeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: findLatest()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the maxTotalReward field returned by findLatest() to calculate the exact cap on total rewards for the current period, then claim rewards in a way that exhausts the cap before other legitimate stakers, effectively stealing their share of unpaid staking rewards?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkStakeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: findLatest()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the maxStakeRewarded field returned by findLatest() to determine the maximum stake eligible for rewards, then craft staking positions that exactly hit this cap while other stakers are excluded, allowing the attacker to monopolize the unpaid staking reward pool?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkStakeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: findLatest()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the stakingPeriodsStored field returned by findLatest() to determine how many historical staking periods are retained, then identify gaps in stored periods where reward calculations may be incorrect, and claim rewards for periods where the system cannot accurately verify the attacker's stake history?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkStakeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: findLatest()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the stakingPeriodDuration field returned by findLatest() to precisely calculate when the current staking period ends, then submit reward claims in the final moments of the period before the network_stake table is updated with new values, claiming rewards based on favorable old parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkStakeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: findLatest()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the stakingPeriod field (mapped as a timestamp range by NetworkStakeMapper) returned by findLatest() to identify the exact boundary between staking periods, then double-claim rewards by submitting transactions that straddle the period boundary, exploiting any off-by-one error in the period range check?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkStakeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: findLatest()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the native SQL query in findLatest() (select * from network_stake where consensus_timestamp = (select max(consensus_timestamp) from network_stake)) which executes two separate table scans without a transaction isolation guarantee, causing a phantom read where a new row is inserted between the inner and outer query execution, returning inconsistent NetworkStake data that inflates reward calculations?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkStakeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: findLatest()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lack of explicit transaction isolation level on the findLatest() native query, causing a non-repeatable read where the network_stake table is modified between the subquery and outer query execution, returning a NetworkStake row with a consensus_timestamp that no longer matches the current maximum, leading to incorrect stakingRewardRate being used for reward calculations?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkStakeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: findLatest()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the network_stake table has no row-level security and findLatest() returns the globally latest record, meaning all users see the same reward parameters regardless of their individual staking context, allowing an attacker to use the exposed unreservedStakingRewardBalance to calculate and claim the maximum possible unpaid rewards before the balance is updated?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkStakeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: findLatest()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the CrudRepository inheritance of NetworkStakeRepository (which extends CrudRepository<NetworkStake, Long>) to invoke inherited methods like findAll() or count() through Spring Data JPA reflection or misconfigured endpoint exposure, enumerating all historical network stake records and extracting reward rate trends to optimize reward theft timing?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkStakeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: findLatest()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the unreservedStakingRewardBalance field returned by findLatest() being a raw long value with no normalization or bounds checking, where a value of 0 or negative (due to data corruption) causes the system to incorrectly compute available rewards, allowing the attacker to claim rewards from the reservedStakingRewards pool that should be protected?
